threads.content:
Everyone says AI will kill video editing jobs. They are wrong.

What AI is doing is splitting one job into two completely different careers.

The mechanical editor who syncs audio, adjusts levels, cuts dead air, and applies standard color grades? That role is 80% automated today. But the creative editor who understands pacing, emotional rhythm, and brand storytelling? Their market value just went up. Way up.

By 2027, 82% of internet traffic will be video. Yet 94% of knowledge workers have zero video production skills.

That gap is the single largest career opportunity I see right now.

I do not mean you need to become a filmmaker. I mean the ability to communicate ideas visually, even approximately, is becoming as fundamental as email literacy was in 2005.

What is one thing you explained in text today that would have been better as video?

instagram.caption:
Video editing agents are eating the boring 80% of post-production work. The creative 20% just became more valuable. What would you automate first? Follow for more on AI media tools.

tweets:
Everyone says AI will kill video editing jobs. They are wrong.
What AI is doing is splitting one job into two completely different careers.

The mechanical editor who syncs audio, adjusts levels, cuts dead air, and applies standard color grades? That role is 80% automated today. But the creative editor who understands pacing, emotional rhythm, and brand storytelling? Their market value just went up. Way up.

I know three editors who tripled their rates in the last year by positioning themselves as "creative directors" who use AI for execution. They now review AI output and make the 5-6 creative decisions that actually move the needle.
The volume of video content brands need is exploding. The demand for creative taste is following. What is disappearing is demand for manual labor disguised as creative work.

If your entire skill set is "I know Premiere Pro really well," you are in trouble. If your skill set is "I know what makes a video feel right," you are entering the best market of your career.
Which side of the split are you on?

twitter.content: Video editing agents are eating the boring 80% of post-production work
